steps.title: Spot the implied contrast
steps.content: The passage uses “even though the declaration is explicitly addressed to the Haitian people” to set up a contrast—what we might expect (it's solely for Haitians) versus what the evidence shows (something more complex).

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: The evidence reveals multiple intended audiences: the declaration is written in French (international diplomatic language, not the first language of most Haitians), includes reassurance to the United States, and positions Haiti as an example for future anti-colonial struggles. These details show the document was designed to communicate with foreign powers and future revolutionaries, not just Haitians. The other choices discuss the declaration's sources (A), external reactions (B), or unsupported claims about Haitian opposition (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
